name: Stéphane Grappelli
description:
'''Stéphane Grappelli''' ({{IPA|fr|stefan ɡʁapɛli}}; 26 January 1908 – 1 December 1997) was a French [[Jazz violin|jazz violinist]]. He is best known as a founder of the [[Quintette du Hot Club de France]] with guitarist [[Django Reinhardt]] in 1934. It was one of the first all-string jazz bands. He has been called "the grandfather of jazz violinists" and continued playing concerts around the world well into his eighties.

For the first three decades of his career, he was billed using a gallicised spelling of his last name, ''Grappelly'', reverting to the Italian spelling ''Grappelli'' in 1969. The latter is used when referring to the violinist, including reissues of his early work.
